profile.name: Deniz Yılmaz
profile.title: Creative Frontend Developer
profile.tagline: Crafting sun-kissed digital experiences from the Aegean coast.
profile.about: I'm a frontend developer who believes great interfaces feel like a gentle sea breeze — effortless, refreshing, and impossible to forget. I work remotely from Bodrum, building products for clients around the world while the Aegean whispers outside my window.

projects.title: Aegean Rentals
projects.description: A villa booking platform for the Turkish Riviera. Built with Vue 3 and a headless CMS, featuring real-time availability and a dreamy photo gallery.

projects.title: Mavi Design System
projects.description: A pastel-forward component library inspired by the blues of the Bodrum coast. Includes 40+ accessible components with dark mode support.

projects.title: Sundown Analytics
projects.description: Real-time dashboard for boutique hotels. Visualizes occupancy, revenue and guest satisfaction with animated D3.js charts.

projects.title: Olive & Salt
projects.description: E-commerce for a local artisan food brand. From branding to checkout — a full Shopify custom theme with hand-coded animations.

blog.title: Why I Moved My Studio to Bodrum (and Never Looked Back)
blog.excerpt: Remote work changed everything. Here's how swapping a city office for an Aegean terrace transformed my creativity and productivity.

blog.title: Building Accessible Color Palettes for Sun-Drenched UIs
blog.excerpt: Pastel colors are tricky for contrast ratios. Here's my tested system for beautiful AND accessible light-themed interfaces.

blog.title: Vue 3 Composables I Reach For Every Project
blog.excerpt: Five composables I've refined over two years that handle auth, form state, infinite scroll and more — ready to copy and adapt.

blog.title: The Minimalist's Guide to CSS Architecture
blog.excerpt: Less is more. I break down the layered approach I use to keep stylesheets clean, scalable and free of specificity wars.
